mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high PAYEMS regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112.5 signals strengthening manufacturing activity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO > 112.5 fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,500 thousand units reflects elevated demand for residential construction, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST > 1500 fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and CPI (CPIAUCSL) signal aggressive monetary tightening amid inflation, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 5% and CPIAUCSL > 210 fail to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC > -0.03 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4.5% indicates a tight labor market that pressures wage growth and corporate margins, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 4.5 fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC > -0.03 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 11,500 billion dollars reflects sustained economic growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 > 11500 fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 112.5 combined with a 5-month increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 11,500 billion dollars signals synchronized supply-side expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO > 112.5 and GDPC1 > 11500 fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases or Rank IC < 0.03 in rolling windows.
